name: Winston-Salem/Forsyth County Schools
about: Winston-Salem/Forsyth County Schools serves approximately 55,000 students in Winston-Salem and Forsyth County, North Carolina. The district is the fourth-largest in the state and part of the Piedmont Triad metropolitan area.
calendarNotes: Winston-Salem/Forsyth County Schools serves the Winston-Salem metropolitan area in North Carolina's Piedmont Triad and follows the state's standard academic calendar — starting in early August and including a Fall Break in mid-October. Spring Break typically falls in late March to early April, broadly aligned with neighboring Guilford County. Families coordinating schedules with Guilford or Alamance County relatives should verify exact dates, as they can differ by a day or two between districts. Winston-Salem has a significant arts and medical community, and the district's calendar reflects a traditional two-semester structure. The city's convenient location between Charlotte and Greensboro on I-40 makes it a common stopping point during holiday travel.
